rulings.Case Title: ROBERT HARR et al VS. VICTORIA MEWS ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Request For Admissions Admitted Pursuant To Ccp 2033.280(B) And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 16, 2010, Line 1. DEFENDANT VICTORIA MEWS ASSOCIATION's (Amended) Motion To Deem Request For Admissions Admitted Pursuant To Ccp 2033.280(B) And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Deny request to deem admitted because Plaintiff provided responses prior to the hearing. Defendant to recover $825 as a reasonable sanction. See CCP 2033.280(c). =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: GOLDSTEIN, GELLMAN, MELBOSTAD, GIBSON & HARRIS LLP VS. ANDREW EHRENFELD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Petition And Petition For Order To Arbitrate And For Dismissal Or Stay Of Action Pending Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 16, 2010, line 7. CROSS DEFENDANT WAUKEEN MCCOY'S Petition For Order To Arbitrate And For Dismissal Or Stay Of Action Pending Arbitration is denied. This action involves a third party and claims not subject to arbitration and there is a possibility of conflicting rulings on common issues of law or fact. C.C.P. section 1281.2(c). =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: EDITH MAZZAFERRI VS. RONALD MAZZAFERRO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion To Have Concurrent Hearings On The Essentially Indentical Motions To Dismiss For Lack Of Subject Matter Jurisdiction Filed And Notice Separte By Four Different Defts
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 16, 2010, line 9. PLAINTIFF EDITH MAZZAFERRI'S Amended Motion To Have Concurrent Hearings On The Essentially Indentical Motions To Dismiss For Lack Of Subject Matter Jurisdiction Filed And Notice Separte By Four Different Defendants. Motion granted pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 128(a), inherent powers of the court. The Great Sunset motion currently set for September 24, 2010; the Beste motion currently set for October 15, 2010; the VanZandt motion currently set for November 24, 2010; the Mazzafferro motion currently set for December 29, 2010; and the Mazzafferro, Trustee motion currently set for January 20, 2011 will all be heard on October 21, 2010 at 9:30 am. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: RITA O'FLYNN VS. BEYOND CHRON (A CA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY) et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 16, 2010, line 10. DEFENDANTS RANDY SHAW, BEYONDCHRON'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is off calendar in view of ruling on special motion to strike. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: RITA O'FLYNN VS. BEYOND CHRON (A CA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY) et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Special Motion To Strike Complaint [Ccp 425.16]
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 16, 2010, line 10. DEFENDANTS RANDY SHAW, BEYONDCHRON'S Special Motion To Strike Complaint [C.C.P. 425.16] is granted, no opposition filed. Plaintiff cannot avoid an anti-SLAPP motion by amending the complaint prior to the hearing on the motion. Plaintiff has submitted no evidence demonstrating a probability of prevailing if allowed to amend to complaint. (See Lam v. Cao (2009) 171 C.A. 4th 858). =(302/CWW)
